taskDescription: Drag the correct terms into the blanks to complete statements about the orbit, extraocular muscles, and lacrimal apparatus.
textField:
The mnemonic LR6 SO4 rest 3 means the lateral rectus is supplied by CN *VI*, the superior oblique by CN *IV*, and all remaining extraocular muscles by CN III (the *oculomotor* nerve). The orbit contains seven bones forming its walls.

Horner's syndrome presents with the triad of *ptosis*, *miosis*, and anhidrosis on the affected side. It results from disruption of the *sympathetic* pathway to the eye at any level from the hypothalamus to the orbit.

The common tendinous ring from which the four recti muscles arise is called the *annulus of Zinn*. The superior oblique muscle passes through a fibrocartilaginous pulley called the *trochlea* before inserting on the eyeball.

In oculomotor (CN III) palsy, the eye is deviated *down and out* due to the unopposed actions of lateral rectus and superior oblique. The pupil is *dilated* and fixed because of loss of parasympathetic supply to the sphincter pupillae.

Tears drain from the lacrimal puncta into lacrimal canaliculi, then the lacrimal sac, and finally the *nasolacrimal* duct which opens into the inferior meatus of the nose.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the anatomy of the orbit and extraocular muscles.
Good effort! Review the details you missed.
